goal: Suggest preprocessing and feature-engineering actions for forecasting meter_reading from the official ASHRAE building and weather tables under the RMSLE metric.

task_characteristics.analysis_notes: Large building-energy forecasting task with a 41.7M-row official test table, a 20.2M-row training table, one building lookup, and split weather lookups keyed by site and timestamp. The benchmark-relevant official column union is only 17, so the task is narrow but operationally heavy. The curated bank keeps 11 rewarded actions across core and model-specific stages; the primary denominator stays conservative around joins, datetime handling, categorical treatment, and missing-value handling.

dataset_description.summary: Official scope combines one main meter-reading table with building metadata and weather lookups. The benchmark uses only Kaggle-provided CSVs and excludes leak tables, holiday calendars, and public-kernel side inputs.
dataset_description.raw_text: train.csv and test.csv provide building-meter-timestamp rows. building_metadata.csv supplies site_id, primary_use, square_feet, year_built, and floor_count by building_id. weather_train.csv and weather_test.csv supply weather signals by site_id and timestamp. sample_submission.csv is the submission index. The benchmark contract is official-only and treats the split train/test weather lookup as a task-local workflow assumption rather than as separate benchmark scenarios.
